title: Well-ordering principle for the naturals
informal_statement: Every nonempty set of natural numbers has a least element: for any nonempty s : Set ℕ, there exists n in s such that n <= m for every m in s.
informal_proof_idea: Take any witness n0 in s, then strong-induct on n0. In the inductive step for n (with hypothesis n in s and ih: every smaller element of s that also lies in s has a minimum below it), either n is already a lower bound for all of s (done), or some k in s is strictly less than n, in which case the induction hypothesis applied to k produces the minimum.
formal_statement_pp: theorem well_ordering (s : Set ℕ) (hs : s.Nonempty) : ∃ n ∈ s, ∀ m ∈ s, n ≤ m

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 96e95cc1), kernel_verified on the second Solve attempt. The first attempt used the same tactic script under the default flat_tactic_sequence transport and kernel-failed: the two `·` bullets after `by_cases` lost their case-block association, leaving the 'neg' branch's goal unsolved (a tactic-transport hazard also hit on the id_bijective and image_union packets in packets/elementary/functions/). Fixed by resubmitting the identical script under proof_format: raw_lean_block, which preserves the bullets' relative indentation. Closes the well_ordering backlog item in packets/elementary/number_theory/QUEUE.md.
